text: When you find that your code isn't working as expected, the first step is identifying bugs. Look carefully at error messages and unexpected outputs. A systematic approach helps you stay organised: check one part of your code at a time rather than guessing randomly. Debugging tools like breakpoints and step-through features let you pause the program and examine variables. Logging is another useful strategy — by printing values to the console at key points, you can trace how data changes. Combining these techniques saves time and makes fixing problems much easier.
